{
  "uiTreePath": ".arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json",
  "dataModel": {
    "entities": [
      {
        "name": "Pet",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true, "generated": "uuid" },
          { "name": "name", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "avatar", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "species", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "breed", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "birthday", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "gender", "type": "string", "enum": ["male", "female", "unknown"], "required": false },
          { "name": "weight", "type": "number", "required": false },
          { "name": "neutered", "type": "boolean", "required": false },
          { "name": "medicalHistory", "type": "string", "required": false }
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "Record",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true, "generated": "uuid" },
          { "name": "petId", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "type", "type": "string", "enum": ["food", "waste", "weight", "medicine"], "required": true },
          { "name": "datetime", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "foodAmount", "type": "number", "required": false },
          { "name": "foodType", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "wasteType", "type": "string", "enum": ["pee", "poop", "vomit", "other"], "required": false },
          { "name": "weightValue", "type": "number", "required": false },
          { "name": "medicineName", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "medicineDose", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "note", "type": "string", "required": false }
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "Reminder",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true, "generated": "uuid" },
          { "name": "petId", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "title", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "category", "type": "string", "enum": ["vaccine", "deworm", "checkup", "other"], "required": true },
          { "name": "dueDate", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "advanceDays", "type": "number", "required": false, "default": 1 },
          { "name": "repeat", "type": "boolean", "required": false, "default": false },
          { "name": "enabled", "type": "boolean", "required": false, "default": true }
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "MedicalRecord",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true, "generated": "uuid" },
          { "name": "petId", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "date", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "hospital", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "doctor", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "symptom", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "diagnosis", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "prescription", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "images", "type": "string[]", "required": false }
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "FamilyGroup",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true, "generated": "uuid" },
          { "name": "name", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "inviteCode", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "members", "type": "FamilyMember[]", "required": true }
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "FamilyMember",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true, "generated": "uuid" },
          { "name": "nickname", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "role", "type": "string", "enum": ["owner", "member"], "required": true },
          { "name": "avatar", "type": "string", "required": false }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "settings": {
      "selectedPetId": { "type": "string", "persist": false },
      "activeTab": { "type": "TabId", "persist": false },
      "currentSurface": { "type": "AppSurface", "persist": false },
      "sheetMode": { "type": "SheetMode | null", "persist": false },
      "editingRecordId": { "type": "string | null", "persist": false },
      "editingPetId": { "type": "string | null", "persist": false },
      "editingReminderId": { "type": "string | null", "persist": false },
      "editingMedicalId": { "type": "string | null", "persist": false },
      "recordFilter": {
        "type": "object",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "petId", "type": "string | 'all'" },
          { "name": "type", "type": "RecordType | 'all'" },
          { "name": "dateRange", "type": "string", "enum": ["7", "30", "90", "all"] }
        ]
      },
      "reminderFilterStatus": { "type": "string", "enum": ["all", "due", "upcoming", "normal"] },
      "medicalFilterPetId": { "type": "string | 'all'" },
      "chart": {
        "type": "object",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "metric", "type": "string", "enum": ["weight", "food", "waste", "medicine"] },
          { "name": "period", "type": "string", "enum": ["week", "month"] },
          { "name": "petId", "type": "string" }
        ]
      }
    },
    "computedState": [
      { "name": "pets", "binding": "/pets", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "records", "binding": "/records", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "reminders", "binding": "/reminders", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "medicalRecords", "binding": "/medical", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "familyGroup", "binding": "/family", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "todayReminders", "binding": "/reminders/today", "dependsOn": ["reminders", "selectedPetId"] },
      { "name": "recentRecords", "binding": "/records/recent", "dependsOn": ["records", "selectedPetId"] },
      { "name": "filteredRecords", "binding": "/records", "dependsOn": ["records", "recordFilter"] },
      { "name": "filteredReminders", "binding": "/reminders", "dependsOn": ["reminders", "reminderFilterStatus"] },
      { "name": "filteredMedical", "binding": "/medical", "dependsOn": ["medicalRecords", "medicalFilterPetId"] },
      { "name": "chartData", "binding": "/chart/data", "dependsOn": ["records", "chart.metric", "chart.period", "chart.petId"] },
      { "name": "chartInsight", "binding": "/chart/insight", "dependsOn": ["chartData"] }
    ]
  },
  "services": [
    {
      "name": "Store",
      "responsibility": "Local JSON persistence using @kit.LocalStorageKit dataPreferences or in-memory fallback. Loads and saves pets, records, reminders, medical records, family group.",
      "api": ["loadAll()", "savePets(pets)", "saveRecords(records)", "saveReminders(reminders)", "saveMedical(medical)", "saveFamily(family)"]
    },
    {
      "name": "Repository",
      "responsibility": "High-level data access and simple query helpers: CRUD for pets/records/reminders/medical/family; latest weight; status classification; chart aggregation.",
      "api": ["add/update/delete/get/list/filter/chartData"]
    },
    {
      "name": "NotificationService",
      "responsibility": "Request publish permission and schedule local notifications for reminders. Stubbed initially; real notificationManager used when API reference available.",
      "api": ["requestPermission()", "scheduleReminder(reminder)", "cancelReminder(id)", "publishDueNotifications(reminders)"]
    },
    {
      "name": "ImageService",
      "responsibility": "Pick image from album/photo picker and return local URI/path. Save medical images to app local files. Stubbed initially.",
      "api": ["pickImage()", "pickImages(max)", "saveMedicalImage(uri)"]
    }
  ],
  "stateMachine": {
    "states": [
      { "name": "boot", "description": "App initializing; load store and hydrate state" },
      { "name": "home", "description": "Main tab surface; selected pet, today reminders, quick records, recent records" },
      { "name": "records", "description": "Records tab with filters and timeline" },
      { "name": "charts", "description": "Charts tab with metric/period selectors" },
      { "name": "profile", "description": "Profile tab with navigation rows" },
      { "name": "pets", "description": "Non-tab surface: pet archive list" },
      { "name": "pet-detail", "description": "Non-tab surface: single pet overview" },
      { "name": "reminders", "description": "Non-tab surface: reminder management list" },
      { "name": "medical", "description": "Non-tab surface: medical records archive" },
      { "name": "family", "description": "Non-tab surface: family group management" },
      { "name": "sheet-open", "description": "A bottom sheet is open; underlying surface remains visible" }
    ],
    "transitions": [
      { "from": "boot", "event": "load-complete", "to": "home" },
      { "from": "home", "event": "switch-tab", "to": "records|charts|profile" },
      { "from": "records", "event": "switch-tab", "to": "home|charts|profile" },
      { "from": "charts", "event": "switch-tab", "to": "home|records|profile" },
      { "from": "profile", "event": "navigate-*", "to": "pets|reminders|medical|family" },
      { "from": "home", "event": "navigate-records", "to": "records" },
      { "from": "pets", "event": "navigate-pet-detail", "to": "pet-detail" },
      { "from": "pet-detail", "event": "navigate-back", "to": "profile|home" },
      { "from": "pets|reminders|medical|family", "event": "navigate-back", "to": "profile" },
      { "from": "*", "event": "open-*-editor", "to": "sheet-open" },
      { "from": "sheet-open", "event": "close-sheet|save-*|delete-*", "to": "previous-surface" }
    ]
  },
  "interactionFlows": [
    {
      "name": "add-pet",
      "trigger": "home-add-pet-button | pets-add-button | pet-detail-edit",
      "steps": [
        "Open pet editor sheet with empty form and optional petId",
        "User fills name (required), species, breed, birthday, gender, weight, neutered, medicalHistory; optionally picks avatar",
        "Validate name non-empty",
        "Save to Repository; persist via Store",
        "If no selected pet, set selectedPetId to new pet",
        "Close sheet and refresh list"
      ],
      "errorPaths": ["empty-name → show inline validation"],
      "sideEffects": ["persist pets"]
    },
    {
      "name": "delete-pet",
      "trigger": "pet-editor-delete",
      "steps": [
        "Delete pet, its records, reminders, and medical records from Repository",
        "Clear selectedPetId if deleted",
        "Persist and close sheet"
      ],
      "errorPaths": []
    },
    {
      "name": "quick-record",
      "trigger": "home-quick-record-* | home-fab-add | records-add-button | records-item-{id}",
      "steps": [
        "Open record editor sheet; if editing existing id, load it; if new, prefill type and petId",
        "User selects pet, type, datetime, type-specific fields, note",
        "Validate required fields per type (petId, type, datetime; weight requires weightValue; medicine requires name)",
        "Save to Repository; persist via Store",
        "Close sheet; refresh home/records/charts"
      ],
      "errorPaths": ["missing-required → inline validation"]
    },
    {
      "name": "delete-record",
      "trigger": "record-editor-delete",
      "steps": ["Delete record from Repository", "Persist and close sheet"]
    },
    {
      "name": "manage-reminder",
      "trigger": "home-reminder-* | pet-detail-add-reminder | reminders-add-button | reminders-item-{id}",
      "steps": [
        "Open reminder editor sheet; load existing if editing",
        "User selects pet, title, category, dueDate, advanceDays, repeat, enabled",
        "Validate petId, title, category, dueDate",
        "Save to Repository; persist; schedule/cancel local notification",
        "Close sheet"
      ],
      "errorPaths": ["missing-required → inline validation"]
    },
    {
      "name": "delete-reminder",
      "trigger": "reminder-editor-delete",
      "steps": ["Delete reminder; cancel notification", "Persist and close sheet"]
    },
    {
      "name": "manage-medical",
      "trigger": "pet-detail-add-medical | medical-add-button | medical-card-{id}",
      "steps": [
        "Open medical editor sheet; load existing if editing",
        "User selects pet, date, hospital, doctor, symptom, diagnosis, prescription; picks up to 9 images",
        "Save to Repository; persist image URIs",
        "Close sheet"
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "delete-medical",
      "trigger": "medical-editor-delete",
      "steps": ["Delete medical record", "Persist and close sheet"]
    },
    {
      "name": "filter-records",
      "trigger": "records-filter-pet | records-filter-type | records-filter-date-range | records-clear-filters",
      "steps": ["Update recordFilter state", "Recompute filteredRecords"]
    },
    {
      "name": "chart-switch",
      "trigger": "charts-metric-* | charts-period-* | charts-pet-switcher",
      "steps": ["Update chart state metric/period/petId", "Recompute chartData and chartInsight"]
    },
    {
      "name": "family-flow",
      "trigger": "profile-family-row | family-create-button | family-invite-button",
      "steps": [
        "If no family group, create one with default name and generated inviteCode",
        "Open family invite sheet showing inviteCode",
        "Copy code to clipboard or share intent"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "testTargets": {
    "home": ["home-pet-switcher", "home-pet-chip-{id}", "home-add-pet-button", "home-reminder-list", "home-reminder-{id}", "home-reminder-empty", "home-quick-record-food", "home-quick-record-waste", "home-quick-record-weight", "home-quick-record-medicine", "home-recent-record-list", "home-recent-record-{id}", "home-view-all-records", "home-tab-bar", "home-tab-records", "home-tab-charts", "home-tab-profile", "home-fab-add"],
    "records": ["records-filter-pet", "records-filter-type", "records-filter-date-range", "records-clear-filters", "records-list", "records-item-{id}", "records-empty", "records-add-button"],
    "charts": ["charts-pet-switcher", "charts-metric-tabs", "charts-metric-weight", "charts-metric-food", "charts-metric-waste", "charts-metric-medicine", "charts-period-week", "charts-period-month", "charts-canvas", "charts-empty", "charts-insight-card"],
    "profile": ["profile-pets-row", "profile-reminders-row", "profile-medical-row", "profile-family-row", "profile-settings-row"],
    "pets": ["pets-list", "pets-card-{id}", "pets-add-button", "pets-empty"],
    "pet-detail": ["pet-detail-back", "pet-detail-edit", "pet-detail-avatar", "pet-detail-name", "pet-detail-info", "pet-detail-latest-weight", "pet-detail-reminder-list", "pet-detail-medical-list", "pet-detail-add-reminder", "pet-detail-add-medical"],
    "reminders": ["reminders-filter-status", "reminders-list", "reminders-item-{id}", "reminders-item-{id}-toggle", "reminders-add-button", "reminders-empty"],
    "medical": ["medical-filter-pet", "medical-list", "medical-card-{id}", "medical-card-{id}-images", "medical-add-button", "medical-empty"],
    "family": ["family-group-name", "family-member-list", "family-member-{id}", "family-invite-button", "family-create-button", "family-empty"],
    "record-editor-sheet": ["record-editor-close", "record-editor-type-picker", "record-editor-pet-picker", "record-editor-datetime", "record-editor-food-amount", "record-editor-food-type", "record-editor-waste-type", "record-editor-weight-value", "record-editor-medicine-name", "record-editor-medicine-dose", "record-editor-note", "record-editor-delete", "record-editor-save"],
    "pet-editor-sheet": ["pet-editor-close", "pet-editor-avatar-picker", "pet-editor-name", "pet-editor-species", "pet-editor-breed", "pet-editor-birthday", "pet-editor-gender", "pet-editor-weight", "pet-editor-neutered", "pet-editor-medical-history", "pet-editor-delete", "pet-editor-save"],
    "reminder-editor-sheet": ["reminder-editor-close", "reminder-editor-pet-picker", "reminder-editor-title", "reminder-editor-category", "reminder-editor-due-date", "reminder-editor-advance-days", "reminder-editor-repeat", "reminder-editor-delete", "reminder-editor-save"],
    "medical-editor-sheet": ["medical-editor-close", "medical-editor-pet-picker", "medical-editor-date", "medical-editor-hospital", "medical-editor-doctor", "medical-editor-symptom", "medical-editor-diagnosis", "medical-editor-prescription", "medical-editor-image-picker", "medical-editor-image-list", "medical-editor-delete", "medical-editor-save"],
    "family-invite-sheet": ["family-invite-close", "family-invite-code", "family-invite-copy", "family-invite-share"]
  },
  "changeRequests": [
    "TabTypes.ets currently defines tab-home, tab-explore, tab-orders, tab-rewards. It must be updated to tab-home, tab-records, tab-charts, tab-profile to match ui-tree.json tabIds.",
    "Index.ets currently imports generic pages (HomePage, ExplorePage, OrdersPage, RewardsPage). It must be updated to import PawLog pages (HomePage, RecordsPage, ChartsPage, ProfilePage) and non-tab surfaces (PetsPage, PetDetailPage, RemindersPage, MedicalPage, FamilyPage).",
    "BottomTabBar.ets TABS data uses generic labels/icons. It must be updated to 今日/记录/趋势/我的 with matching icons.",
    "Theme.ets currently uses cafe/gold palette. It must be updated to mint-green + paw-pink palette per spec.",
    "SvgIcon.ets variant mapping currently uses beverage/cafe icons. It must be updated to pet/home/chart/profile/paw medical/reminder icons once SVG assets are available.",

          { "name": "doctor", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "symptom", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "diagnosis", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "prescription", "type": "string", "required": false },
          { "name": "images", "type": "string[]", "required": false }
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "FamilyGroup",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true, "generated": "uuid" },
          { "name": "name", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "inviteCode", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "members", "type": "FamilyMember[]", "required": true }
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "FamilyMember",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "id", "type": "string", "required": true, "generated": "uuid" },
          { "name": "nickname", "type": "string", "required": true },
          { "name": "role", "type": "string", "enum": ["owner", "member"], "required": true },
          { "name": "avatar", "type": "string", "required": false }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "settings": {
      "selectedPetId": { "type": "string", "persist": false },
      "activeTab": { "type": "TabId", "persist": false },
      "currentSurface": { "type": "AppSurface", "persist": false },
      "sheetMode": { "type": "SheetMode | null", "persist": false },
      "editingRecordId": { "type": "string | null", "persist": false },
      "editingPetId": { "type": "string | null", "persist": false },
      "editingReminderId": { "type": "string | null", "persist": false },
      "editingMedicalId": { "type": "string | null", "persist": false },
      "recordFilter": {
        "type": "object",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "petId", "type": "string | 'all'" },
          { "name": "type", "type": "RecordType | 'all'" },
          { "name": "dateRange", "type": "string", "enum": ["7", "30", "90", "all"] }
        ]
      },
      "reminderFilterStatus": { "type": "string", "enum": ["all", "due", "upcoming", "normal"] },
      "medicalFilterPetId": { "type": "string | 'all'" },
      "chart": {
        "type": "object",
        "fields": [
          { "name": "metric", "type": "string", "enum": ["weight", "food", "waste", "medicine"] },
          { "name": "period", "type": "string", "enum": ["week", "month"] },
          { "name": "petId", "type": "string" }
        ]
      }
    },
    "computedState": [
      { "name": "pets", "binding": "/pets", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "records", "binding": "/records", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "reminders", "binding": "/reminders", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "medicalRecords", "binding": "/medical", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "familyGroup", "binding": "/family", "source": "store" },
      { "name": "todayReminders", "binding": "/reminders/today", "dependsOn": ["reminders", "selectedPetId"] },
      { "name": "recentRecords", "binding": "/records/recent", "dependsOn": ["records", "selectedPetId"] },
      { "name": "filteredRecords", "binding": "/records", "dependsOn": ["records", "recordFilter"] },
      { "name": "filteredReminders", "binding": "/reminders", "dependsOn": ["reminders", "reminderFilterStatus"] },
      { "name": "filteredMedical", "binding": "/medical", "dependsOn": ["medicalRecords", "medicalFilterPetId"] },
      { "name": "chartData", "binding": "/chart/data", "dependsOn": ["records", "chart.metric", "chart.period", "chart.petId"] },
      { "name": "chartInsight", "binding": "/chart/insight", "dependsOn": ["chartData"] }
    ]
  },
  "services": [
    {
      "name": "Store",
      "responsibility": "Local JSON persistence using @kit.LocalStorageKit dataPreferences or in-memory fallback. Loads and saves pets, records, reminders, medical records, family group.",
      "api": ["loadAll()", "savePets(pets)", "saveRecords(records)", "saveReminders(reminders)", "saveMedical(medical)", "saveFamily(family)"]
    },
    {
      "name": "Repository",
      "responsibility": "High-level data access and simple query helpers: CRUD for pets/records/reminders/medical/family; latest weight; status classification; chart aggregation.",
      "api": ["add/update/delete/get/list/filter/chartData"]
    },
    {
      "name": "NotificationService",
      "responsibility": "Request publish permission and schedule local notifications for reminders. Stubbed initially; real notificationManager used when API reference available.",
      "api": ["requestPermission()", "scheduleReminder(reminder)", "cancelReminder(id)", "publishDueNotifications(reminders)"]
    },
    {
      "name": "ImageService",
      "responsibility": "Pick image from album/photo picker and return local URI/path. Save medical images to app local files. Stubbed initially.",
      "api": ["pickImage()", "pickImages(max)", "saveMedicalImage(uri)"]
    }
  ],
  "stateMachine": {
    "states": [
      { "name": "boot", "description": "App initializing; load store and hydrate state" },
      { "name": "home", "description": "Main tab surface; selected pet, today reminders, quick records, recent records" },
      { "name": "records", "description": "Records tab with filters and timeline" },
      { "name": "charts", "description": "Charts tab with metric/period selectors" },
      { "name": "profile", "description": "Profile tab with navigation rows" },
      { "name": "pets", "description": "Non-tab surface: pet archive list" },
      { "name": "pet-detail", "description": "Non-tab surface: single pet overview" },
      { "name": "reminders", "description": "Non-tab surface: reminder management list" },
      { "name": "medical", "description": "Non-tab surface: medical records archive" },
      { "name": "family", "description": "Non-tab surface: family group management" },
      { "name": "sheet-open", "description": "A bottom sheet is open; underlying surface remains visible" }
    ],
    "transitions": [
      { "from": "boot", "event": "load-complete", "to": "home" },
      { "from": "home", "event": "switch-tab", "to": "records|charts|profile" },
      { "from": "records", "event": "switch-tab", "to": "home|charts|profile" },
      { "from": "charts", "event": "switch-tab", "to": "home|records|profile" },
      { "from": "profile", "event": "navigate-*", "to": "pets|reminders|medical|family" },
      { "from": "home", "event": "navigate-records", "to": "records" },
      { "from": "pets", "event": "navigate-pet-detail", "to": "pet-detail" },
      { "from": "pet-detail", "event": "navigate-back", "to": "profile|home" },
      { "from": "pets|reminders|medical|family", "event": "navigate-back", "to": "profile" },
      { "from": "*", "event": "open-*-editor", "to": "sheet-open" },
      { "from": "sheet-open", "event": "close-sheet|save-*|delete-*", "to": "previous-surface" }
    ]
  },
  "interactionFlows": [
    {
      "name": "add-pet",
      "trigger": "home-add-pet-button | pets-add-button | pet-detail-edit",
      "steps": [
        "Open pet editor sheet with empty form and optional petId",
        "User fills name (required), species, breed, birthday, gender, weight, neutered, medicalHistory; optionally picks avatar",
        "Validate name non-empty",
        "Save to Repository; persist via Store",
        "If no selected pet, set selectedPetId to new pet",
        "Close sheet and refresh list"
      ],
      "errorPaths": ["empty-name → show inline validation"],
      "sideEffects": ["persist pets"]
    },
    {
      "name": "delete-pet",
      "trigger": "pet-editor-delete",
      "steps": [
        "Delete pet, its records, reminders, and medical records from Repository",
        "Clear selectedPetId if deleted",
        "Persist and close sheet"
      ],
      "errorPaths": []
    },
    {
      "name": "quick-record",
      "trigger": "home-quick-record-* | home-fab-add | records-add-button | records-item-{id}",
      "steps": [
        "Open record editor sheet; if editing existing id, load it; if new, prefill type and petId",
        "User selects pet, type, datetime, type-specific fields, note",
        "Validate required fields per type (petId, type, datetime; weight requires weightValue; medicine requires name)",
        "Save to Repository; persist via Store",
        "Close sheet; refresh home/records/charts"
      ],
      "errorPaths": ["missing-required → inline validation"]
    },
    {
      "name": "delete-record",
      "trigger": "record-editor-delete",
      "steps": ["Delete record from Repository", "Persist and close sheet"]
    },
    {
      "name": "manage-reminder",
      "trigger": "home-reminder-* | pet-detail-add-reminder | reminders-add-button | reminders-item-{id}",
      "steps": [
        "Open reminder editor sheet; load existing if editing",
        "User selects pet, title, category, dueDate, advanceDays, repeat, enabled",
        "Validate petId, title, category, dueDate",
        "Save to Repository; persist; schedule/cancel local notification",
        "Close sheet"
      ],
      "errorPaths": ["missing-required → inline validation"]
    },
    {
      "name": "delete-reminder",
      "trigger": "reminder-editor-delete",
      "steps": ["Delete reminder; cancel notification", "Persist and close sheet"]
    },
    {
      "name": "manage-medical",
      "trigger": "pet-detail-add-medical | medical-add-button | medical-card-{id}",
      "steps": [
        "Open medical editor sheet; load existing if editing",
        "User selects pet, date, hospital, doctor, symptom, diagnosis, prescription; picks up to 9 images",
        "Save to Repository; persist image URIs",
        "Close sheet"
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "delete-medical",
      "trigger": "medical-editor-delete",
      "steps": ["Delete medical record", "Persist and close sheet"]
    },
    {
      "name": "filter-records",
      "trigger": "records-filter-pet | records-filter-type | records-filter-date-range | records-clear-filters",
      "steps": ["Update recordFilter state", "Recompute filteredRecords"]
    },
    {
      "name": "chart-switch",
      "trigger": "charts-metric-* | charts-period-* | charts-pet-switcher",
      "steps": ["Update chart state metric/period/petId", "Recompute chartData and chartInsight"]
    },
    {
      "name": "family-flow",
      "trigger": "profile-family-row | family-create-button | family-invite-button",
      "steps": [
        "If no family group, create one with default name and generated inviteCode",
        "Open family invite sheet showing inviteCode",
        "Copy code to clipboard or share intent"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "testTargets": {
    "home": ["home-pet-switcher", "home-pet-chip-{id}", "home-add-pet-button", "home-reminder-list", "home-reminder-{id}", "home-reminder-empty", "home-quick-record-food", "home-quick-record-waste", "home-quick-record-weight", "home-quick-record-medicine", "home-recent-record-list", "home-recent-record-{id}", "home-view-all-records", "home-tab-bar", "home-tab-records", "home-tab-charts", "home-tab-profile", "home-fab-add"],

    "profile": ["profile-pets-row", "profile-reminders-row", "profile-medical-row", "profile-family-row", "profile-settings-row"],
    "pets": ["pets-list", "pets-card-{id}", "pets-add-button", "pets-empty"],

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/arkui-ui-patterns

# ArkUI UI Patterns

## Overview

Use this skill before writing ArkTS UI code. Its job is to turn a user request, design artifact, or implementation task into an ArkUI-native implementation plan, so HarmonyOS screens start from known component patterns instead of trial-and-error compilation fixes.

## Local Reference Roots

When a reference points to local HarmonyOS docs or verified sample code, resolve these environment variables instead of hard-coded machine paths:

- `HARMONY_KNOWLEDGE_BASE_ROOT`: repository root for the HarmonyOS knowledge base; docs are expected under `$HARMONY_KNOWLEDGE_BASE_ROOT/data/harmonyos_docs`.
- `HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT`: repository root for the local HarmonyOS sample app used for verified implementation examples.

## Required Workflow

1. Classify the screen shape from the user prompt, design artifact, nearby project examples, or implementation plan.
2. Open `references/components-index.md`.
3. Load only the reference files that match the screen shape.
4. Produce a short `ArkUI Pattern Plan` before editing `.ets` files.
5. Write or refactor the ArkTS implementation using the loaded patterns.
6. Validate with the project build and a focused HarmonyOS smoke check when runtime access is available.

## ArkUI Pattern Plan

Include this plan in your implementation reasoning before generating code:

```text
ArkUI Pattern Plan
- App shell:
- Navigation:
- Tabs:
- Repeated content:
- Search/filter:
- Scroll/grid layout:
- Forms and controls:
- Dialogs and overlays:
- State ownership:
- Async/loading:
- Theme/accessibility:
- Data/client:
- Component split:
- Verification:
- References loaded:
```

Keep the plan concise. It should decide the ArkUI primitives and state ownership before code is emitted, not restate the visual design.

## Default Decisions

- Prefer ArkUI-native structure over direct HTML/CSS translation.
- For scaffolded bottom-navigation apps in this repository, use the fixed shell built from `common/Theme.ets`, `common/SvgIcon.ets`, `common/TabTypes.ets`, `components/BottomTabBar.ets`, and `pages/Index.ets`.
- If the scaffold already provides `common/Theme.ets`, `common/SvgIcon.ets`, `common/TabTypes.ets`, `components/BottomTabBar.ets`, and `pages/Index.ets` as a fixed bottom-tab shell, preserve that shell contract unless the user explicitly asks for a navigation rewrite.
- Prefer `NavPathStack` and `navDestination` over `@ohos.router` unless an existing project already requires router pages.
- Use `ListItem` or `ListItemGroup` as the direct list item shape. Use `LazyForEach` or `Repeat` for large, unknown, or frequently updated data.
- Define named model classes, enums, and parameter classes. Avoid anonymous object types and untyped dynamic records in UI state.
- Keep `build()` declarative. Put validation, navigation, filtering, sorting, and async work into methods.
- Create app-level services/repositories in the shell or a small dependency graph. Pass broad shared data explicitly or with `@Provide`/`@Consume`; keep feature-local clients as component parameter inputs.
- For tabbed apps with drill-down flows in each tab, decide whether the product needs a shared root history or one `NavPathStack` per tab before writing pages.
- Protect async state with request tokens or lifecycle guards when search, refresh, tab changes, or page dismissal can race with pending work.
- Add stable component ids or accessibility labels when the project needs UI testing or screen-reader support.

## High-Risk Anti-Patterns

- Generating a full ArkTS page from a raw visual description without first choosing navigation, list, dialog, and state patterns.
- Placing every screen and all feature logic in `Index.ets`.
- Calling navigation stack operations from `aboutToAppear`.
- Rewriting an existing fixed `BottomTabBar.ets` scaffold into native `Tabs + TabContent` without a concrete product reason.
- Bypassing `BottomTabBar.ets`, `Theme.ets`, or `SvgIcon.ets` when the scaffold already uses them as shared shell contracts.
- Using `ForEach` without a stable key generator.
- Creating multiple sibling components from one `LazyForEach` item generator.
- Nesting same-axis scroll containers without explicit sizing.
- Doing network calls, expensive filtering, sorting, or object construction inside `build()`, `ForEach`, `LazyForEach`, `keyGenerator`, or `getData`.
- Managing dialogs with many unrelated booleans instead of a single dialog state or controller.
- Letting stale async results overwrite newer query/filter/refresh state.
- Using one shared tab navigation stack when users expect each tab to keep its own back history.
- Passing a mutable `ViewModel` into child pages as `vm!` or a plain field and expecting `vm.field = value` to refresh UI.
- Binding live editable controls like `Slider` or `TextInput` to plain builder parameters or plain fields and expecting adjacent labels to refresh. Use `@State`, `@Link`, or `$$` binding, and write new values to reactive state before service or store calls.

## Reference Map

Start with `references/components-index.md`, then load the smallest useful set:

- `references/app-wiring.md` for root page layout, app shell, dependency graph, service ownership, and generated app file boundaries.
- `references/project-structure.md` for choosing minimal, feature-lite, or modular feature file layout before creating pages, view models, repositories, and shared helpers.
- `references/navigation.md` for multi-screen flows, detail pages, editors, per-tab stacks, and route parameters.
- `references/tabs.md` for 2-4 primary destinations and tab history ownership.
- `references/list-and-lazy-data.md` for rows, feeds, sections, search results, and history.
- `references/scrollview.md` for custom scrolling, horizontal strips, scroll controllers, and scroll nesting.
- `references/grids.md` for icon pickers, media galleries, responsive grids, and WaterFlow-like collections.
- `references/search-and-filter.md` for native Search, local filters, async search, scopes, and stale result handling.
- `references/forms-and-controls.md` for settings, editors, filters, and input-heavy pages.
- `references/controls.md` for choosing Button, Toggle, Checkbox, Slider, Select, Radio, and segmented controls.
- `references/live-slider-control.md` for sliders with nearby live values, settings rows, and reusable slider row components.
- `references/focus-and-keyboard.md` for input focus, keyboard submit, field chaining, and keyboard-safe pages.
- `references/input-toolbar.md` for chat/composer screens with bottom-anchored input.
- `references/dialogs-and-overlays.md` for confirmations, pickers, custom modals, popups, and transient feedback.
- `references/overlays-and-feedback.md` for toasts, banners, progress overlays, menus, and non-modal feedback.
- `references/state-ownership.md` for choosing `@State`, `@Prop`, `@Link`, `@Observed`, `@ObjectLink`, `@Provide`, and `@Consume`.
- `references/async-state.md` for loading, empty, error, refresh, search states, lifecycle guards, and stale async result handling.
- `references/loading-placeholders.md` for loading indicators, placeholders, empty states, and retry surfaces.
- `references/lightweight-clients.md` for small repository/client/service boundaries, JSON preferences stores, and persistence shims in generated apps.
- `references/media.md` for Image, Video, thumbnails, viewers, and media-heavy rows.
- `references/theming.md` for semantic tokens, dark mode, `WithTheme`, and system resource colors.
- `references/accessibility.md` for accessibility text, grouping, roles, ids, and screen reader-safe custom rows.
- `references/component-structure.md` for splitting pages into reusable components and builders.
- `references/performance.md` for first-pass performance constraints that prevent later UI rewrites.
- `references/verification.md` for build, smoke, state propagation, and failure-repair checks after implementation.

Provide concise pattern recommendations with code shape.

content.content:
1	# Components Index
2	
3	Use this index to choose the smallest set of ArkUI pattern references before writing code.
4	
5	## Available Components
6	
7	- New app, generated app shell, shared repositories/services, view models, or multi-page task: load `project-structure.md`, `app-wiring.md`, and `navigation.md`.
8	- Bottom navigation, primary destinations, dashboard sections, or per-tab detail history: load `app-wiring.md`, `tabs.md`, and `navigation.md`.
9	- Existing scaffold with `Theme.ets`, `SvgIcon.ets`, `TabTypes.ets`, `BottomTabBar.ets`, and `Index.ets`: preserve that shell contract unless the user asks for a navigation rewrite.
10	- Feed, todo list, settings list, history, search results, gallery list, or repeated rows: load `list-and-lazy-data.md`.
11	- Custom scroll regions, horizontal chips, mixed sections, sticky-like surfaces, or nested scrolls: load `scrollview.md`.
12	- Icon picker, photo grid, dashboard tiles, responsive columns, or masonry-like layout: load `grids.md`.
13	- Search box, query scopes, filters, typeahead, or result refresh: load `search-and-filter.md`.
14	- Create/edit/settings pages: load `forms-and-controls.md`, `controls.md`, `state-ownership.md`, and `dialogs-and-overlays.md` when a picker, sheet, or confirmation is present.
15	- Slider settings, numeric range rows, or live text beside a slider: load `live-slider-control.md`.
16	- Input-heavy page, field chaining, keyboard submit, or keyboard-safe layout: load `focus-and-keyboard.md`.
17	- Chat, composer, command box, or bottom action input: load `input-toolbar.md`.
18	- Confirm delete, modal edit, picker, or sheet-like interaction: load `dialogs-and-overlays.md`.
19	- Toast, banner, non-modal progress, contextual popup, or menu feedback: load `overlays-and-feedback.md`.
20	- Image, video, thumbnail strip, gallery, or media viewer: load `media.md`.
21

# Components Index

Use this index to choose the smallest set of ArkUI pattern references before writing code.

## Available Components

- New app, generated app shell, shared repositories/services, view models, or multi-page task: load `project-structure.md`, `app-wiring.md`, and `navigation.md`.
- Bottom navigation, primary destinations, dashboard sections, or per-tab detail history: load `app-wiring.md`, `tabs.md`, and `navigation.md`.
- Existing scaffold with `Theme.ets`, `SvgIcon.ets`, `TabTypes.ets`, `BottomTabBar.ets`, and `Index.ets`: preserve that shell contract unless the user asks for a navigation rewrite.
- Feed, todo list, settings list, history, search results, gallery list, or repeated rows: load `list-and-lazy-data.md`.
- Custom scroll regions, horizontal chips, mixed sections, sticky-like surfaces, or nested scrolls: load `scrollview.md`.
- Icon picker, photo grid, dashboard tiles, responsive columns, or masonry-like layout: load `grids.md`.
- Search box, query scopes, filters, typeahead, or result refresh: load `search-and-filter.md`.
- Create/edit/settings pages: load `forms-and-controls.md`, `controls.md`, `state-ownership.md`, and `dialogs-and-overlays.md` when a picker, sheet, or confirmation is present.
- Slider settings, numeric range rows, or live text beside a slider: load `live-slider-control.md`.
- Input-heavy page, field chaining, keyboard submit, or keyboard-safe layout: load `focus-and-keyboard.md`.
- Chat, composer, command box, or bottom action input: load `input-toolbar.md`.
- Confirm delete, modal edit, picker, or sheet-like interaction: load `dialogs-and-overlays.md`.
- Toast, banner, non-modal progress, contextual popup, or menu feedback: load `overlays-and-feedback.md`.
- Image, video, thumbnail strip, gallery, or media viewer: load `media.md`.

## Cross-Cutting References

- Network, local storage, search, refresh, empty result, error handling, debounce, or stale result protection: load `async-state.md`.
- Loading skeleton, empty state, error state, retry, or progress button: load `loading-placeholders.md`.
- Generated mock data, repository/service boundary, preferences persistence, local CRUD store, persistence shim, API-like client, or feature directory split: load `project-structure.md` and `lightweight-clients.md`.
- Theme tokens, dark mode, semantic colors, or typography: load `theming.md`.
- Screen reader behavior, custom rows, icon-only buttons, or testable labels: load `accessibility.md`.
- Large page, many repeated sections, or refactor request: load `component-structure.md`.
- More than about 30 rows, frequently changing data, nested scrolls, or expensive render logic: load `performance.md`.
- Any ArkTS implementation before completion: load `verification.md`.

## Default App Shape

For ordinary-user app generation, start from:

```text
Fixed BottomTabBar Shell
  Index shell
    Feature pages and reusable sections
  non-tab surface replacement when needed
```

Use this shape unless the app is genuinely single-screen and has no detail, edit, settings, or secondary flow. If the scaffold already provides `Theme.ets`, `SvgIcon.ets`, `TabTypes.ets`, `BottomTabBar.ets`, and `Index.ets`, keep those file roles stable and adapt the content inside the shell.

When a task combines local data, filters/forms, and detail navigation, load only these first: `project-structure.md`, `app-wiring.md`, `navigation.md`, `tabs.md`, `state-ownership.md`, `lightweight-clients.md`, and `dialogs-and-overlays.md` if modals are needed.

## Source Basis

These patterns are derived from the local HarmonyOS documentation under `$HARMONY_KNOWLEDGE_BASE_ROOT/data/harmonyos_docs`, especially ArkUI component references for Navigation, Tabs, List, Scroll, Grid, Search, ForEach, LazyForEach, CustomDialog, WithTheme, accessibility attributes, component ids, and state/rendering control.

## Adding Entries

- Add the component file and link it here with a short "when to use" description.
- Keep each component reference short, pattern-oriented, and grounded in HarmonyOS docs or verified project examples.

1	# App Wiring Pattern
2	
3	Use this when generating the root page or deciding file boundaries for a HarmonyOS app.
4	
5	## Intent
6	
7	Make `Index.ets` an app shell, not the whole app. The shell owns navigation, top-level tab state, app-level service/model instances, and route registration. Feature pages own their local UI state.
8	
9	## Fixed BottomTabBar Shell
10	
11	When the scaffold already provides a fixed bottom-tab shell through files such as:
12	
13	```text
14	entry/src/main/ets/common/Theme.ets
15	entry/src/main/ets/common/SvgIcon.ets
16	entry/src/main/ets/common/TabTypes.ets
17	entry/src/main/ets/components/BottomTabBar.ets
18	entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets
19	```
20	
21	use this shell as the default app-wiring pattern for scaffolded bottom-tab apps.
22	
23	In this shape:
24	
25	- `pages/Index.ets` stays the app shell and owns `activeTab`, current surface, and non-tab surface replacement.
26	- `components/BottomTabBar.ets` stays the reusable fixed bottom bar with a stable `activeTab` / `onTap` API.
27	- `common/TabTypes.ets` stays the shared `TabId` source of truth.
28	- `common/Theme.ets` stays the semantic token layer for shell spacing, colors, and tab styling.
29	- `common/SvgIcon.ets` stays the shared icon wrapper; update variant mapping there instead of scattering rawfile icon paths through feature pages.
30	
31	Use this shell when:
32	
33	- the scaffold already includes it
34	- the product has 2-4 primary destinations
35	- tab surfaces are rendered inline and non-tab surfaces behave like full-page replacements or overlays
36	- the user did not explicitly ask for a different router architecture or a navigation rewrite
37	
38	```ts
39	import { Theme } from '../common/Theme'
40	import { TabId } from '../common/TabTypes'
41	import { BottomTabBar } from '../components/BottomTabBar'
42	
43	@Entry
44	@Component
45	struct Index {
46	  @State private activeTab: TabId = 'tab-home'
47	  @State private surface: string = 'home'
48	
49	  build() {
50	    Stack() {
51	      Stack({ alignContent: Alignment.Bottom }) {
52	        this.CurrentSurface()
53	
54	        if (this.isTabSurface(this.surface)) {
55	          BottomTabBar({
56	            activeTab: this.activeTab,
57	            onTap: (tab: TabId): void => this.goToTab(tab)
58	          })
59	        }
60	      }
61	    }
62	    .backgroundColor(Theme.pageBg)
63	  }
64	}
65	```
66	
67	Keep this shell conservative: change page imports, tab metadata, surface wiring, theme tokens, and icon mappings as needed, but keep the file roles stable unless the user asks for an architectural change.
68	
69	## Dependency Graph
70	
71	Create long-lived repositories, clients, and app stores at the app shell or in a small graph class. Pages should own transient UI state such as selected filters, draft text, and loading phase; services should own data policy such as fixtures, persistence, request shaping, and cache refresh.
72	
73	```ts
74	export class AppServices {
75	  readonly itemRepository: ItemRepository = new ItemRepository()
76	  readonly settingsRepository: SettingsRepository = new SettingsRepository()
77	}
78	```
79	
80	Use explicit parameters for feature-local dependencies. Use `@Provide`/`@Consume` only for broad shared state that many descendants genuinely need, such as a selected account, app settings, or a root `NavPathStack`. Do not put every repository in `AppStorage`.
81	
82	## AppStorage And LocalStorage
83	
84	- Use `LocalStorage` for state scoped to one UIAbility or entry page.
85	- Use `AppStorage` for small app-level values that must cross UIAbility boundaries or receive values from Ability lifecycle code.
86	- Keep services and clients as plain class instances. Store only serializable flags, ids, and user preferences in storage.
87	- When `onNewWant` or other Ability callbacks seed navigation state through `AppStorage`, read and clear that value from a page lifecycle such as `onPageShow` before pushing a route.
88	
89	## File Boundaries
90	
91	- `pages/Index.ets`: entry shell, root navigation, app-level route builder.
92	- `pages/*Page.ets`: full-screen pages and route destinations.
93	- `components/*.ets`: reusable sections, rows, cards, form rows, empty states.
94	- `models/*.ets`: named model classes, route parameter classes, enums.
95	- `services/*.ets`: data loading, persistence, generated mock data, adapters, app dependency graph.
96	- `common/*.ets` or `theme/*.ets`: tokens, constants, shared helpers.
97	- When using a fixed bottom-tab shell, keep `common/Theme.ets`, `common/SvgIcon.ets`, `common/TabTypes.ets`, `components/BottomTabBar.ets`, and `pages/Index.ets` as the shell boundary.
98	
99	## Keep
100	
101	- Keep root state small and serializable where practical.
102	- Pass `NavPathStack` only to components that need to push or pop.
103	- Put route names and route parameter classes in named exports.
104	- Keep generated fake data in a service or model file, not inline in `build()`.
105	- Keep app services stable for the lifetime of the shell; do not recreate repositories inside child page `build()` methods.
106	- Use mock repositories with the same public methods as later real clients so generated pages do not need structural rewrites.
107	- If the scaffold already includes a fixed `BottomTabBar.ets`, preserve its API and move tab metadata or icon mapping changes into the shell files instead of duplicating bottom navigation in feature pages.
108	- Keep theme-token changes inside `Theme.ets` and shared icon mapping changes inside `SvgIcon.ets` when the scaffold already provides them.
109	
110	## Avoid
111	
112	- All generated screens inside `Index.ets`.
113	- Multiple independent `Navigation` roots unless the product requires separate navigation contexts.
114	- Calling navigation stack methods from `aboutToAppear`; wait until the page is built and use event handlers or explicit user actions.
115	- Passing component instances or large mutable UI objects as route parameters.
116	- Module-level mutable singletons for feature state unless the existing project already uses that pattern.
117	- `AppStorage`/`LocalStorage` as a dumping ground for repositories, controllers, or large model graphs.
118	- Replacing an existing fixed `BottomTabBar.ets` shell with a different root architecture when the scaffold contract already matches the requested product shape.
119	- Hardcoding rawfile icon paths across feature pages when the scaffold already provides `common/SvgIcon.ets`.
120	
121	## Source Notes
122	
123	HarmonyOS docs describe `AppStorage` as global application state and `LocalStorage` as state local to one UIAbility. They are useful for app/UIAbility data synchronization, while repositories and generated service clients should remain plain ArkTS classes unless they need ArkUI observation.
124

file.content:
# App Wiring Pattern

Use this when generating the root page or deciding file boundaries for a HarmonyOS app.

## Intent

Make `Index.ets` an app shell, not the whole app. The shell owns navigation, top-level tab state, app-level service/model instances, and route registration. Feature pages own their local UI state.

## Fixed BottomTabBar Shell

When the scaffold already provides a fixed bottom-tab shell through files such as:

```text
entry/src/main/ets/common/Theme.ets
entry/src/main/ets/common/SvgIcon.ets
entry/src/main/ets/common/TabTypes.ets
entry/src/main/ets/components/BottomTabBar.ets
entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets
```

use this shell as the default app-wiring pattern for scaffolded bottom-tab apps.

In this shape:

- `pages/Index.ets` stays the app shell and owns `activeTab`, current surface, and non-tab surface replacement.
- `components/BottomTabBar.ets` stays the reusable fixed bottom bar with a stable `activeTab` / `onTap` API.
- `common/TabTypes.ets` stays the shared `TabId` source of truth.
- `common/Theme.ets` stays the semantic token layer for shell spacing, colors, and tab styling.
- `common/SvgIcon.ets` stays the shared icon wrapper; update variant mapping there instead of scattering rawfile icon paths through feature pages.

Use this shell when:

- the scaffold already includes it
- the product has 2-4 primary destinations
- tab surfaces are rendered inline and non-tab surfaces behave like full-page replacements or overlays
- the user did not explicitly ask for a different router architecture or a navigation rewrite

```ts
import { Theme } from '../common/Theme'
import { TabId } from '../common/TabTypes'
import { BottomTabBar } from '../components/BottomTabBar'

@Entry
@Component
struct Index {
  @State private activeTab: TabId = 'tab-home'
  @State private surface: string = 'home'

  build() {
    Stack() {
      Stack({ alignContent: Alignment.Bottom }) {
        this.CurrentSurface()

        if (this.isTabSurface(this.surface)) {
          BottomTabBar({
            activeTab: this.activeTab,
            onTap: (tab: TabId): void => this.goToTab(tab)
          })
        }
      }
    }
    .backgroundColor(Theme.pageBg)
  }
}
```

Keep this shell conservative: change page imports, tab metadata, surface wiring, theme tokens, and icon mappings as needed, but keep the file roles stable unless the user asks for an architectural change.

## Dependency Graph

Create long-lived repositories, clients, and app stores at the app shell or in a small graph class. Pages should own transient UI state such as selected filters, draft text, and loading phase; services should own data policy such as fixtures, persistence, request shaping, and cache refresh.

```ts
export class AppServices {
  readonly itemRepository: ItemRepository = new ItemRepository()
  readonly settingsRepository: SettingsRepository = new SettingsRepository()
}
```

Use explicit parameters for feature-local dependencies. Use `@Provide`/`@Consume` only for broad shared state that many descendants genuinely need, such as a selected account, app settings, or a root `NavPathStack`. Do not put every repository in `AppStorage`.

## AppStorage And LocalStorage

- Use `LocalStorage` for state scoped to one UIAbility or entry page.
- Use `AppStorage` for small app-level values that must cross UIAbility boundaries or receive values from Ability lifecycle code.
- Keep services and clients as plain class instances. Store only serializable flags, ids, and user preferences in storage.
- When `onNewWant` or other Ability callbacks seed navigation state through `AppStorage`, read and clear that value from a page lifecycle such as `onPageShow` before pushing a route.

## File Boundaries

- `pages/Index.ets`: entry shell, root navigation, app-level route builder.
- `pages/*Page.ets`: full-screen pages and route destinations.
- `components/*.ets`: reusable sections, rows, cards, form rows, empty states.
- `models/*.ets`: named model classes, route parameter classes, enums.
- `services/*.ets`: data loading, persistence, generated mock data, adapters, app dependency graph.
- `common/*.ets` or `theme/*.ets`: tokens, constants, shared helpers.
- When using a fixed bottom-tab shell, keep `common/Theme.ets`, `common/SvgIcon.ets`, `common/TabTypes.ets`, `components/BottomTabBar.ets`, and `pages/Index.ets` as the shell boundary.

## Keep

- Keep root state small and serializable where practical.
- Pass `NavPathStack` only to components that need to push or pop.
- Put route names and route parameter classes in named exports.
- Keep generated fake data in a service or model file, not inline in `build()`.
- Keep app services stable for the lifetime of the shell; do not recreate repositories inside child page `build()` methods.
- Use mock repositories with the same public methods as later real clients so generated pages do not need structural rewrites.
- If the scaffold already includes a fixed `BottomTabBar.ets`, preserve its API and move tab metadata or icon mapping changes into the shell files instead of duplicating bottom navigation in feature pages.
- Keep theme-token changes inside `Theme.ets` and shared icon mapping changes inside `SvgIcon.ets` when the scaffold already provides them.

## Avoid

- All generated screens inside `Index.ets`.
- Multiple independent `Navigation` roots unless the product requires separate navigation contexts.
- Calling navigation stack methods from `aboutToAppear`; wait until the page is built and use event handlers or explicit user actions.
- Passing component instances or large mutable UI objects as route parameters.
- Module-level mutable singletons for feature state unless the existing project already uses that pattern.
- `AppStorage`/`LocalStorage` as a dumping ground for repositories, controllers, or large model graphs.
- Replacing an existing fixed `BottomTabBar.ets` shell with a different root architecture when the scaffold contract already matches the requested product shape.
- Hardcoding rawfile icon paths across feature pages when the scaffold already provides `common/SvgIcon.ets`.

## Source Notes

HarmonyOS docs describe `AppStorage` as global application state and `LocalStorage` as state local to one UIAbility. They are useful for app/UIAbility data synchronization, while repositories and generated service clients should remain plain ArkTS classes unless they need ArkUI observation.

content.content:
1	# State Ownership Pattern
2	
3	Use this before choosing ArkUI decorators or moving state between pages and components.
4	
5	## Defaults
6	
7	- `@State`: local component-owned reactive state.
8	- `@Prop`: parent-to-child input when the child should not mutate the parent value.
9	- `@Link`: parent-owned value that a child is allowed to mutate.
10	- `@Observed` with `@ObjectLink`: object state whose property changes must refresh child UI.
11	- `@Provide` and `@Consume`: section-wide or app-wide shared dependencies. Use sparingly.
12	- Plain private fields: constants, controllers, services, and derived helpers that do not need to trigger UI refresh.
13	
14	## Generated Model Rules
15	
16	Use named classes with defaults:
17	
18	```ts
19	@Observed
20	export class TodoItem {
21	  id: string = ''
22	  title: string = ''
23	  completed: boolean = false
24	}
25	```
26	
27	Avoid anonymous object types in component state:
28	
29	```ts
30	// Avoid for generated ArkTS UI state.
31	@State item: { id: string, title: string } = { id: '', title: '' }
32	```
33	
34	## Parent To Child
35	
36	Read-only child:
37	
38	```ts
39	@Component
40	struct SummaryCard {
41	  @Prop title: string
42	}
43	```
44	
45	Child edits parent primitive:
46	
47	```ts
48	@Component
49	struct SearchBox {
50	  @Link query: string
51	}
52	```
53	
54	Child edits nested object:
55	
56	```ts
57	@Component
58	struct TodoRow {
59	  @ObjectLink item: TodoItem
60	}
61	```
62	
63	## Ownership Decisions
64	
65	- Page owns selected tab, search query, filters, drafts, loading state, and dialog state.
66	- Row owns only transient row-only UI state unless the data change must affect the list.
67	- Services own persistence, data loading, and generated mock repositories.
68	- Root shell owns navigation and app-wide selection only when multiple pages need it.
69	
70	## ViewModel State Shape
71	
72	For pages with async loading, persistence, filtering, or navigation side effects, use a small state plus view-model boundary. Do not copy a large sample-app inheritance tree unless the project already uses one.
73	
74	For a copyable starting point, read `../snippets/StateViewModelSkeleton.ets`.
75	
76	Use `@State private screenState: ItemListState = this.vm.state` at the page level. Use `@ObjectLink` for row/detail components that must observe nested item changes. If list mutations are simple, reassign arrays (`this.items = next`) rather than relying on `push` or `splice` to refresh child UI.
77	
78	Shared render state rule: if child tabs/pages render fields from a shared object, that object's class must be `@Observed` and children must receive it with `@ObjectLink`; otherwise `object.field = value` may build successfully but fail to re-render other tabs/pages.
79	
80	For tabbed apps where multiple pages read and update the same screen state, read `../snippets/SharedStateTabsShell.ets`. Use this shape when a root shell owns a view model, multiple `TabContent` pages display fields from the same state object, and page button clicks must update UI across tabs. The root keeps the view model as a plain command object, owns `@State private appState = this.vm.state`, and passes the observed state to child pages with `@ObjectLink`. Child pages call callbacks such as `onIncrement` instead of receiving a mutable `vm!`. For small demos, `@Observed ViewModel` is acceptable only when the VM is the rendered shared state object; for larger apps, prefer `@Observed AppState` for render data plus a plain VM/controller for commands, services, and persistence.
81	
82	Do not repair component input initialization errors by changing `vm: SomeViewModel` to `vm!: SomeViewModel` when the UI reads mutable fields from that view model. Pick the state owner first:
83	
84	- If the child only reads immutable input, use `@Prop`.
85	- If the child edits a parent primitive or array reference, use `@Link`.
86	- If the child observes fields on a shared object, make that object `@Observed` and pass it with `@ObjectLink`; do not rely on a parent `@State vm` wrapper to observe `vm.field` mutations.
87	- If the child triggers behavior, pass a defaulted callback or a plain service that does not need to refresh UI.
88	
89	## Form Drafts
90	
91	Keep create/edit drafts local to the page or dialog, then submit to a view model or service. This avoids mutating nested fields on a plain controller object while expecting ArkUI to refresh.
92	
93	```ts
94	@State private draftName: string = ''
95	
96	private saveDraft(): void {
97	  let draft: ItemDraft = new ItemDraft()
98	  draft.name = this.draftName.trim()
99	  this.vm.save(draft)
100	}
101	```
102	
103	## Avoid
104	
105	- Uninitialized decorated state.
106	- Mutating nested properties on a plain object and expecting all child UI to refresh.
107	- Passing a plain mutable ViewModel to tab/page children and expecting derived stats, lists, or badges to refresh after another tab changes it.
108	- Plain `AppState` objects passed through many components without `@Observed`, `@ObjectLink`, `@Link`, or explicit state reassignment.
109	- Plain `vm!` or `viewModel!` fields in child pages when the page renders mutable view-model fields.
110	- Passing mutable arrays into many children without a single update path.
111	- Using `@Provide/@Consume` to avoid one or two explicit parameters.
112	- Global mutable singletons for screen state in generated apps.
113	
114	## Source Notes
115	
116	The local sample app uses `@Observed` state classes and VM objects in files such as `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/features/mine/src/main/ets/viewmodel/NewContentPageState.ets` and `NewContentVM.ets`. Reuse the small `State + VM + event/method` idea; avoid importing its large app-specific inheritance, breakpoint, and status-bar logic into generated apps.
117

# State Ownership Pattern

Use this before choosing ArkUI decorators or moving state between pages and components.

## Defaults

- `@State`: local component-owned reactive state.
- `@Prop`: parent-to-child input when the child should not mutate the parent value.
- `@Link`: parent-owned value that a child is allowed to mutate.
- `@Observed` with `@ObjectLink`: object state whose property changes must refresh child UI.
- `@Provide` and `@Consume`: section-wide or app-wide shared dependencies. Use sparingly.
- Plain private fields: constants, controllers, services, and derived helpers that do not need to trigger UI refresh.

## Generated Model Rules

Use named classes with defaults:

```ts
@Observed
export class TodoItem {
  id: string = ''
  title: string = ''
  completed: boolean = false
}
```

Avoid anonymous object types in component state:

```ts
// Avoid for generated ArkTS UI state.
@State item: { id: string, title: string } = { id: '', title: '' }
```

## Parent To Child

Read-only child:

```ts
@Component
struct SummaryCard {
  @Prop title: string
}
```

Child edits parent primitive:

```ts
@Component
struct SearchBox {
  @Link query: string
}
```

Child edits nested object:

```ts
@Component
struct TodoRow {
  @ObjectLink item: TodoItem
}
```

## Ownership Decisions

- Page owns selected tab, search query, filters, drafts, loading state, and dialog state.
- Row owns only transient row-only UI state unless the data change must affect the list.
- Services own persistence, data loading, and generated mock repositories.
- Root shell owns navigation and app-wide selection only when multiple pages need it.

## ViewModel State Shape

For pages with async loading, persistence, filtering, or navigation side effects, use a small state plus view-model boundary. Do not copy a large sample-app inheritance tree unless the project already uses one.

For a copyable starting point, read `../snippets/StateViewModelSkeleton.ets`.

Use `@State private screenState: ItemListState = this.vm.state` at the page level. Use `@ObjectLink` for row/detail components that must observe nested item changes. If list mutations are simple, reassign arrays (`this.items = next`) rather than relying on `push` or `splice` to refresh child UI.

Shared render state rule: if child tabs/pages render fields from a shared object, that object's class must be `@Observed` and children must receive it with `@ObjectLink`; otherwise `object.field = value` may build successfully but fail to re-render other tabs/pages.

For tabbed apps where multiple pages read and update the same screen state, read `../snippets/SharedStateTabsShell.ets`. Use this shape when a root shell owns a view model, multiple `TabContent` pages display fields from the same state object, and page button clicks must update UI across tabs. The root keeps the view model as a plain command object, owns `@State private appState = this.vm.state`, and passes the observed state to child pages with `@ObjectLink`. Child pages call callbacks such as `onIncrement` instead of receiving a mutable `vm!`. For small demos, `@Observed ViewModel` is acceptable only when the VM is the rendered shared state object; for larger apps, prefer `@Observed AppState` for render data plus a plain VM/controller for commands, services, and persistence.

Do not repair component input initialization errors by changing `vm: SomeViewModel` to `vm!: SomeViewModel` when the UI reads mutable fields from that view model. Pick the state owner first:

- If the child only reads immutable input, use `@Prop`.
- If the child edits a parent primitive or array reference, use `@Link`.
- If the child observes fields on a shared object, make that object `@Observed` and pass it with `@ObjectLink`; do not rely on a parent `@State vm` wrapper to observe `vm.field` mutations.
- If the child triggers behavior, pass a defaulted callback or a plain service that does not need to refresh UI.

## Form Drafts

Keep create/edit drafts local to the page or dialog, then submit to a view model or service. This avoids mutating nested fields on a plain controller object while expecting ArkUI to refresh.

```ts
@State private draftName: string = ''

private saveDraft(): void {
  let draft: ItemDraft = new ItemDraft()
  draft.name = this.draftName.trim()
  this.vm.save(draft)
}
```

## Avoid

- Uninitialized decorated state.
- Mutating nested properties on a plain object and expecting all child UI to refresh.
- Passing a plain mutable ViewModel to tab/page children and expecting derived stats, lists, or badges to refresh after another tab changes it.
- Plain `AppState` objects passed through many components without `@Observed`, `@ObjectLink`, `@Link`, or explicit state reassignment.
- Plain `vm!` or `viewModel!` fields in child pages when the page renders mutable view-model fields.
- Passing mutable arrays into many children without a single update path.
- Using `@Provide/@Consume` to avoid one or two explicit parameters.
- Global mutable singletons for screen state in generated apps.

## Source Notes

The local sample app uses `@Observed` state classes and VM objects in files such as `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/features/mine/src/main/ets/viewmodel/NewContentPageState.ets` and `NewContentVM.ets`. Reuse the small `State + VM + event/method` idea; avoid importing its large app-specific inheritance, breakpoint, and status-bar logic into generated apps.

1	# Lightweight Clients Pattern
2	
3	Use this for generated apps that need mock data, local repositories, persistence shims, or small API-like services without a heavy architecture.
4	
5	## Intent
6	
7	Keep views declarative and keep data policy outside `build()`. Generated apps should have a small repository/client layer even when the data is mocked.
8	
9	## Minimal Shape
10	
11	```ts
12	export class ItemRepository {
13	  private items: ItemModel[] = []
14	
15	  constructor() {
16	    this.items = this.createInitialItems()
17	  }
18	
19	  listItems(): ItemModel[] {
20	    return this.items.slice()
21	  }
22	
23	  search(query: string, scope: SearchScope): ItemModel[] {
24	    let normalized: string = query.trim().toLowerCase()
25	    return this.items.filter((item: ItemModel) => {
26	      return item.title.toLowerCase().includes(normalized)
27	    })
28	  }
29	
30	  private createInitialItems(): ItemModel[] {
31	    return [
32	      new ItemModel('1', 'First item'),
33	      new ItemModel('2', 'Second item')
34	    ]
35	  }
36	}
37	```
38	
39	## JSON Preferences Store
40	
41	Use this for small local CRUD/settings data. Pass `Context` from a page or app shell; do not require global `AppStorage` for a generated small app.
42	
43	For a copyable starting point, read `../snippets/JsonPreferenceStore.ets`.
44	
45	Map parsed JSON into named model classes instead of exposing dynamic records to UI state:
46	
47	```ts
48	private toItem(raw: Record<string, string | number | boolean>): ItemModel {
49	  let item: ItemModel = new ItemModel()
50	  item.id = String(raw['id'] ?? '')
51	  item.title = String(raw['title'] ?? '')
52	  item.createdAt = Number(raw['createdAt'] ?? 0)
53	  return item
54	}
55	
56	async list(): Promise<ItemModel[]> {
57	  let json: string = this.store.getString('items', '[]')
58	  try {
59	    return (JSON.parse(json) as object[]).map((raw: object) => {
60	      return this.toItem(raw as Record<string, string | number | boolean>)
61	    })
62	  } catch (_) {
63	    return []
64	  }
65	}
66	```
67	
68	## Page Usage
69	
70	```ts
71	private repository: ItemRepository = new ItemRepository()
72	@State private items: ItemModel[] = []
73	
74	aboutToAppear(): void {
75	  if (this.items.length === 0) {
76	    this.items = this.repository.listItems()
77	  }
78	}
79	```
80	
81	## Rules
82	
83	- Keep repositories/services as plain classes unless they need ArkUI observation.
84	- Return copies for arrays when callers should not mutate internal state directly.
85	- Wrap preferences operations in `try/catch`; static checks often flag them as throwing.
86	- Await `flush()` when the surrounding method is async, or intentionally encapsulate it in a fire-and-forget helper with error handling.
87	- Put generated fixtures in repository/model files, not `build()`.
88	- Keep async/network boundaries in services; keep UI phase state in pages or stores.
89	- Use named models and enums for data, not anonymous objects.
90	- Prefer explicit setter methods or full typed model assignment over TypeScript utility types such as `Partial<T>` plus object spread. ArkTS strict checks reject object spread in ordinary object literals.
91	
92	## Avoid
93	
94	- Global mutable arrays as app state.
95	- Fetching, sorting, or generating mock data inside `build()`.
96	- A large view model for a screen with simple local state.
97	- Client classes that know about ArkUI components.
98	- Object spread updates such as `{ ...this.settings, ...patch }` in generated ArkTS services.
99	- `Record<string, unknown>` or `any` in generated ArkTS parsing paths; use explicit DTO/model mapping.
100	
101	## Source Notes
102	
103	This mirrors the SwiftUI lightweight-client role for HarmonyOS: a small swappable data boundary that supports generated apps, tests, and later replacement by real APIs.
104	
105	The local sample app wraps preferences with try/catch in `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/common/src/main/ets/storagemanager/PreferenceManager.ets`. Reuse the protection and JSON boundary, not its fixed singleton, logger, or global UIContext.
106

1	# Dialogs And Overlays Pattern
2	
3	Use this for confirmations, custom editors, pickers, action sheets, popups, menus, and transient feedback.
4	
5	## Decision
6	
7	- Simple confirmation: `AlertDialog`.
8	- List of actions: `ActionSheet` or a menu.
9	- Date/time/text picker: platform picker dialog.
10	- Custom modal content: `CustomDialog`.
11	- Lightweight contextual hint: popup or tips control.
12	- Short feedback: toast/prompt action where available in the project.
13	
14	## Custom Dialog Shape
15	
16	`CustomDialogController` should be a member of a `@Component` and should open a `@CustomDialog` component.
17	
18	```ts
19	@CustomDialog
20	struct RenameDialog {
21	  controller: CustomDialogController
22	  @Link name: string
23	
24	  build() {
25	    Column({ space: 12 }) {
26	      Text('Rename')
27	      TextInput({ text: this.name })
28	        .id('rename-input')
29	        .onChange((value: string) => {
30	          this.name = value
31	        })
32	      Button('Done')
33	        .id('rename-done-button')
34	        .onClick(() => this.controller.close())
35	    }
36	    .padding(20)
37	  }
38	}
39	```
40	
41	```ts
42	@State private draftName: string = ''
43	private renameDialog: CustomDialogController = new CustomDialogController({
44	  builder: RenameDialog({
45	    name: $draftName
46	  })
47	})
48	```
49	
50	## State Model
51	
52	For multiple modal choices, prefer one state field:
53	
54	```ts
55	enum ActiveDialog {
56	  None,
57	  DeleteConfirm,
58	  Rename
59	}
60	```
61	
62	Open dialogs through methods such as `openRename(itemId: string)` and `confirmDelete()`, so state setup stays outside `build()`.
63	
64	## Sheet State
65	
66	For bottom sheets or half-modal editors, keep one boolean or enum as the source of truth and reset it in the dismiss callback.
67	
68	For copyable starting points, read `../snippets/BindSheetEditor.ets` or `../snippets/CustomDialogForm.ets`.
69	
70	If `bindSheet` support is uncertain for the target SDK, use a `CustomDialog` form rather than hand-rolling a full-screen overlay unless the visual contract specifically requires it.
71	
72	## Rules
73	
74	- Dialog dynamic content should use linked or consumed state when it must refresh.
75	- Close an existing custom dialog before replacing a global or shared controller.
76	- Keep destructive confirmation copy short and action-specific.
77	- Add ids to dialog inputs and primary/secondary buttons.
78	
79	## Avoid
80	
81	- Many unrelated booleans such as `showDelete`, `showRename`, `showPicker`.
82	- `@Prop` or `@ObjectLink` as the only path for dialog content that must update while open.
83	- Directly closing the parent dialog from a nested dialog launcher.
84	- Recreating controllers inside `build()`.
85	- Keeping sheet state only in a service or plain controller object when the UI must react to open/close changes.
86	
87	## Source Notes
88	
89	HarmonyOS CustomDialog docs require the controller to be used with `@CustomDialog` and `@Component`, note that controller parameters do not dynamically refresh, and recommend `@Link` or `@Consume` for dynamic dialog content.
90	
91	The local sample app uses `.bindSheet(show, builder, { onWillDisappear })` in `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/features/devpractices/src/main/ets/component/SampleCard.ets`; reuse the state-reset pattern without copying its web-sheet-specific details.
92

file.content:
# Dialogs And Overlays Pattern

Use this for confirmations, custom editors, pickers, action sheets, popups, menus, and transient feedback.

## Decision

- Simple confirmation: `AlertDialog`.
- List of actions: `ActionSheet` or a menu.
- Date/time/text picker: platform picker dialog.
- Custom modal content: `CustomDialog`.
- Lightweight contextual hint: popup or tips control.
- Short feedback: toast/prompt action where available in the project.

## Custom Dialog Shape

`CustomDialogController` should be a member of a `@Component` and should open a `@CustomDialog` component.

```ts
@CustomDialog
struct RenameDialog {
  controller: CustomDialogController
  @Link name: string

  build() {
    Column({ space: 12 }) {
      Text('Rename')
      TextInput({ text: this.name })
        .id('rename-input')
        .onChange((value: string) => {
          this.name = value
        })
      Button('Done')
        .id('rename-done-button')
        .onClick(() => this.controller.close())
    }
    .padding(20)
  }
}
```

```ts
@State private draftName: string = ''
private renameDialog: CustomDialogController = new CustomDialogController({
  builder: RenameDialog({
    name: $draftName
  })
})
```

## State Model

For multiple modal choices, prefer one state field:

```ts
enum ActiveDialog {
  None,
  DeleteConfirm,
  Rename
}
```

Open dialogs through methods such as `openRename(itemId: string)` and `confirmDelete()`, so state setup stays outside `build()`.

## Sheet State

For bottom sheets or half-modal editors, keep one boolean or enum as the source of truth and reset it in the dismiss callback.

For copyable starting points, read `../snippets/BindSheetEditor.ets` or `../snippets/CustomDialogForm.ets`.

If `bindSheet` support is uncertain for the target SDK, use a `CustomDialog` form rather than hand-rolling a full-screen overlay unless the visual contract specifically requires it.

## Rules

- Dialog dynamic content should use linked or consumed state when it must refresh.
- Close an existing custom dialog before replacing a global or shared controller.
- Keep destructive confirmation copy short and action-specific.
- Add ids to dialog inputs and primary/secondary buttons.

## Avoid

- Many unrelated booleans such as `showDelete`, `showRename`, `showPicker`.
- `@Prop` or `@ObjectLink` as the only path for dialog content that must update while open.
- Directly closing the parent dialog from a nested dialog launcher.
- Recreating controllers inside `build()`.
- Keeping sheet state only in a service or plain controller object when the UI must react to open/close changes.

## Source Notes

HarmonyOS CustomDialog docs require the controller to be used with `@CustomDialog` and `@Component`, note that controller parameters do not dynamically refresh, and recommend `@Link` or `@Consume` for dynamic dialog content.

The local sample app uses `.bindSheet(show, builder, { onWillDisappear })` in `$HARMONY_SAMPLE_APP_ROOT/features/devpractices/src/main/ets/component/SampleCard.ets`; reuse the state-reset pattern without copying its web-sheet-specific details.
